title: CloudFront Origins, OAC, SSE-KMS, and Private Delivery · Go Semantics Case: Oac Sourcearn Policy
summary: A distribution points at an S3 website endpoint, retains legacy OAI by habit, permits the CloudFront service principal globally, and forgets the KMS key policy. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the oac sourcearn policy increment in private CloudFront-to-S3 origin contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.
Configure OAC with always-sign where appropriate and define viewer protocol, origin protocol, request signing, and forwarded Authorization behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Oac Sourcearn Policy · Theory Oac Sourcearn Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 1418 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
